ametathetos: unchangeable (occ=2, 2 verses) — Heb 6:17-18: 'the unchangeable character of his purpose... two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie'. Analytically significant as the negative form of the meta- (change) root applied to God: God is ametathetos — his purposes do not change. This provides the theological ground of hope for those who repent: the God to whom they return is unchangeable in his covenantal commitments. 2 verses.

GROUP 2 — MORAL KNOWING. gnōsis — knowledge, understanding. 71 occ, 28 verses. The 1 Cor 8 passage is the central conscience-and-knowledge discourse in the NT: gnōsis (knowledge) and suneidēsis (conscience) are explicitly in dialogue across 1 Cor 8:1-12. 'Knowledge puffs up' (v.1) vs the weak conscience (v.7/10/12) — gnōsis without love/conscience is destructive. Also: 2 Cor 4:6 (light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Christ — moral illumination as conscience-awakening), 2 Cor 10:5 (taking every thought captive to the knowledge of God).

FIRE FAMILY. pur — fire. 507 occ, 69 verses. HIGH_FREQUENCY_ANCHOR. The connection to envy/zeal runs through the Hebrew qin.ah corpus (qin.ah burns like fire: Psa 79:5; Eze 38:19; Zep 1:18; Deu 4:24 — 'consuming fire, a jealous God') but this is a Hebrew semantic. The Greek pur corpus in this export (69 verses) yields only 4 verses with zeal/consume language and none are envy-specific. The zeal-as-fire semantic is fully carried by the Hebrew terms; G4442 adds no analytical value to this registry. Classified candidate_delete pending researcher confirmation.

FIRE FAMILY. puroō — to burn, to be inflamed. 23 occ, 6 verses. 1 Cor 7:9 ('better to marry than to burn with passion') is the closest to an inner-state burning verse — but this is desire/passion, not envy. 2 Cor 11:29 ('who is made to fall and I am not indignant/burning?') — Paul's pastoral burning is closer to concern than envy. Remaining verses are eschatological fire (2 Pet 3:12) and literal/apocalyptic (Rev). The inner-burning sense is present but thin and not envy-specific. PRIMARY NT CORE. phthonos — envy. 9 occ, 10 verses. The purest Greek envy term — ill-will at another's good fortune. Mat 27:18 / Mar 15:10 ANCHOR: Pilate knew it was out of envy that the chief priests delivered Jesus — the only verse in the NT where phthonos is named as the direct cause of the crucifixion. GROUP 1 — CORE. kil.yah — kidneys. 31 occ, 26 verses. The OT anatomical seat of conscience and deepest inner scrutiny. Corpus splits: 13 sacrificial/anatomical verses (Exo/Lev ritual use — kidneys as the innermost fat offered to God, grounding the inner-being theology); 13 conscience-seat verses in Psalms and Prophets: Psa 7:9 (God tests minds/hearts), Psa 16:7 (kidneys instruct in the night), Psa 26:2 (test my heart and mind), Psa 73:21 (pricked in heart — kilyah), Psa 139:13 (you formed my inward parts), Pro 23:16 (my inmost being will exult), Jer 11:20 / 17:10 / 20:12 (God searches heart and kidneys/mind). Full verse set retained — the sacrificial use is the theological ground for the inner-being application.
exclusion_reason: Primary analysis in conscience registry (reg 26). CRL: SHARED_TERM established. Heart registry sufficiently covered by lev/le.vav/kardia for the heart-testing dimension.

THE primary Hebrew heart term. 331 verses with refs. Covers the full semantic range: cognitive centre (lev as the seat of thought, planning, wisdom), moral centre (pure/evil/upright/deceitful heart), volitional centre (the heart that chooses, inclines, hardens), relational centre (hearts knit together, turned away, wholly given). Most concentrated in wisdom literature and Deuteronomic covenant vocabulary. Must be extracted as the registry's anchor term.

status_note: Verbal form of the lev root — to take the heart, captivate, encourage. 5 verses. Song 4:9 (twice) — 'you have captivated my heart, my sister, my bride; you have captivated my heart with one glance of your eyes.' The verbal action of the heart being seized in love. Also 2Sa 13:6 (Amnon's feigned illness to lure Tamar — the manipulation of the heart-captivation dynamic). Job 11:12 (the stupid man who gets understanding). The verb form shows the heart as capable of being acted upon from outside — the relational and vulnerable dimension.

status_note: PRIMARY HEBREW CORE. qa.na — to be jealous, to envy, to be zealous. 34 occ, 29 verses. The root of the entire qana family. Covers: human envy of prosperity (Gen 26:14 Philistines envy Isaac; Gen 30:1 Rachel envies Leah; Gen 37:11 brothers jealous of Joseph; Psa 73:3 envious of the arrogant); marital jealousy (Num 5:14/30); divine jealousy for God's honour (1 Ki 19:10/14 Elijah zealous for the Lord; Num 25:11/13 Phinehas jealous for God; Zec 1:14; 8:2 God jealous for Jerusalem); prohibition of envy in wisdom literature (Psa 37:1; Pro 3:31; 23:17; 24:1/19). GOD_AS_SUBJECT flag present. Dual register (negative envy / positive zeal) is the defining structural feature of this root.

HEBREW CORE. qan.na — Jealous (as divine name/title). 1 occ. Exo 34:14: 'the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.' The divine name El Qanna — jealousy as an attribute so fundamental it functions as a proper name for God. Thin data (1 verse) but analytically irreplaceable.
anchor_note: Framework A/B intersection term. Ontological ground of inner-being peace. Session B analysis deferred until intersection question formally opened.

status_note: HEBREW CORE. qan.na — Jealous (as divine name/title). 1 occ. Exo 34:14: 'the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.' The divine name El Qanna — jealousy as an attribute so fundamental it functions as a proper name for God. Thin data (1 verse) but analytically irreplaceable.

HEBREW CORE. qan.na — jealous (divine adjective). 5 occ, 5 verses. The adjectival form used exclusively of God: Exo 20:5 (Ten Commandments — I am a jealous God); Exo 34:14; Deu 4:24 (a consuming fire, a jealous God — fire and jealousy directly linked in the Hebrew); Deu 5:9; Deu 6:15. GOD_AS_SUBJECT throughout. These are the Decalogue and covenant renewal jealousy clauses — the theological ground of the entire divine-jealousy theme.

GOD_AS_SUBJECT throughout. These are the Decalogue and covenant renewal jealousy clauses — the theological ground of the entire divine-jealousy theme.

PRIMARY HEBREW CORE. qin.ah — jealousy, envy, zeal. 43 occ, 42 verses — the largest verse set in this registry. The noun form of the root. Covers: marital jealousy law (Num 5:14-30 — the ordeal of jealousy, six uses in this passage alone); human destructive jealousy (Job 5:2: jealousy slays the simple; Pro 6:34: jealousy makes a man furious; Pro 14:30 ANCHOR: envy makes the bones rot; Pro 27:4: who can stand before jealousy?; Ecc 4:4: all toil comes from envy; Son 8:6: jealousy fierce as the grave); divine jealousy (Psa 79:5; Eze 5:13; 16:38/42; 23:25; 36:5-6; 38:19; Zep 1:18; 3:8; Zec 1:14; 8:2); divine zeal as positive attribute (2 Ki 19:31; Psa 69:9; 119:139; Isa 9:7; 37:32; 42:13; 59:17; 63:15). GOD_AS_SUBJECT flag. SOMATIC_INNER_LINK flag (bones rot, Pro 14:30).

HEBREW CORE. qan.no — jealous (intensive adjective). 2 occ, 2 verses. Jos 24:19 (Joshua's covenant warning: God is a jealous God, he will not forgive transgressions) and Nah 1:2 (the Lord is a jealous and avenging God). Both are covenantal-judgment contexts — divine jealousy as the ground of moral consequence.
